description_long: This is a classic biplane floatplane with a striking blue and yellow color scheme, featuring a single large upper wing and a slightly smaller lower wing, both with straight edges and rounded tips. The fuselage is streamlined and painted blue, with a yellow vertical stabilizer and rudder at the tail. The airplane is equipped with two large silver floats instead of traditional wheels, allowing it to land on water, and the floats have visible struts connecting them to the fuselage and wings. The engine is a radial type, exposed at the nose, with a propeller mounted at the front. The cockpit is positioned just behind the engine, enclosed with a clear canopy, and appears to be a single-seat configuration. The registration number 'NC47M' is prominently displayed in black on the upper wing.
description: A blue and yellow biplane floatplane with a radial engine, large silver floats, and a single enclosed cockpit. The airplane features straight-edged wings and a classic tail design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue fuselage, yellow upper wing with 'NC47M', and both silver floats clearly attached beneath.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the radial engine, propeller, and the symmetrical placement of the floats under the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar features to view 0, showing the tail and float structure from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear frontal view focusing on the engine, propeller, and the alignment of the floats and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with straight, parallel wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted radial piston engine with exposed cylinders and front propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Twin fixed floats with supporting struts, no wheels visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the engine.
